title: Custom Properties
description: CSS custom properties (variables) store a value under a name prefixed with --. Any property can reference it with var(). Changing the variable updates every use automatically.
challenge: Both elements use var(--brand-color) but the variable isn't defined — they fall back to gray. Define --brand-color on .container and set it to #a855f7.
realWorldUsage: Design systems define a set of custom properties on :root for brand colors, spacing, and border-radius. Every component references these shared values. Theming — dark mode, white-label variants — is then a matter of swapping variables, not rewriting component CSS.
